channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Woman finds suspected explosives on her farm in Isiolo and hands them to police
item.description: Residents of Kambi ya Juu village in Isiolo were left shaken after a 71-year-old woman, Severina Kairigo, brought suspected explosive devices she had found on her farm to the local police post. She di...

item.title: Punjab Forms Joint Investigation Teams to Probe TLP Cases
item.description: LAHORE: The Punjab government, led by Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz, has established 12 Joint Investigation Teams (JITs) to probe cases against the proscribed Tehreek-e-Labbaik Pakistan (TLP). Seven JIT...

item.title: Vice Mayor and Companions Face Charges in Misamis Oriental Woman's Killing
item.description: CAGAYAN DE ORO CITY, Philippines — A municipal vice mayor from Lanao del Sur, together with his companions, has been officially charged in connection with the fatal shooting of a woman and the injury ...

item.title: Australians safely repatriated from Middle East amid regional conflict
item.description: The first group of Australians stranded in the Middle East due to the escalating conflict have been successfully repatriated to Sydney, marking the beginning of a larger operation to bring home the re...

item.title: Inclusive design enhances societal resilience during crises
item.description: The article explores the concept of inclusive design and how designing with people who face barriers benefits everyone. It begins with the well-known 'curb-cut effect,' where small modifications to as...

item.title: Third Way Centrists Discuss 2028 Strategy Amid Iran War Divisions
item.description: Centrist Democrats gathered at a Third Way conference to strategize for the 2028 presidential election, focusing initially on domestic issues like affordability and elevating moderate voices online. T...

item.title: Madurai High Court allows more time for temple board to respond on Karthigai Deepam prayer arrangement
item.description: The Madurai Bench of the Madras High Court has granted additional time to the Board of Trustees of the Subramaniya Swamy Temple at Thirupparankundram to deliberate on a court suggestion related to the...

item.title: U.S.-Israeli Strikes Sink Iranian Warship Near Sri Lanka Amid Escalating West Asia Conflict
item.description: On 4th March 2026, a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship, IRIS Dena, in the Indian Ocean near Sri Lanka, resulting in the death of at least 83 people. U.S. Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed ...

item.title: Government Launches Fully Digital Portal for National Highway Access Road Approvals
item.description: The Government of India has introduced a fully digital system for approving access roads from National Highways (NHs), eliminating the need for citizens and businesses to physically visit government o...

item.title: Tebogo Malope to bring Turfloop student resistance story to the big screen
item.description: Multi-award-winning South African director Tebogo Malope is set to return to his roots with a new feature film, 'Studying Under the Barrel of a Gun,' which chronicles the courageous student resistance...

item.title: France Rejects Niger Junta's Claims Over Airport Attack Amid Tensions
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron don waka come out clear say France no get hand for the Niger airport attack wey happen on 29 January, na the one wey Islamic State for Sahel claim. Dis come after Nige...

item.title: Joke Silva Shares Story of Her Marriage to Olu Jacobs
item.description: Nollywood actress Joke Silva recently talk about her long-lasting marriage to veteran actor Olu Jacobs during her appearance on TVC’s Your View. She yarn say their love story begin on top a movie set,...

item.title: CDF Munir stresses Taliban must end support for terrorist groups for Pak-Afghan peace
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces and Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir visited Wana in South Waziristan on Wednesday to review security and operational readiness along Pakistan's western border. He emphasise...

item.title: Exploring the Experience of Using AI as a Therapeutic Tool
item.description: Rhik Samadder, a self-proclaimed AI sceptic, documented his experience using artificial intelligence as a substitute for traditional therapy in The Guardian's 'AI for the People' series. Over six week...

item.title: Discovery of Rare Corpse Flower in Apayao Highlights Forest Conservation
item.description: A rare ‘corpse flower’ known as Rafflesia lagascae has been discovered in the forest of Calanasan, Apayao, coinciding with the celebration of World Wildlife Day. The small, saucer-sized parasitic flow...

item.title: APC Explains Why E No Get Board Of Trustees
item.description: The National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Professor Nentawe Yilwatda, don explain why the party dey operate without Board of Trustees. E talk say na because the party wan make memb...

item.title: Understanding African Colonialism through Archaeology
item.description: Colonialism has played a major role in shaping societies globally, and Africa is no exception. Most studies focus on European colonialism in the 19th and 20th centuries, but recent research in the jou...

item.title: Val Morgan Digital staff informed of $10.5 million acquisition by Vinyl Group via media leak
item.description: Staff at Val Morgan Digital, a subsidiary of cinema operator Hoyts, discovered that their company was being acquired by ASX-listed Vinyl Group through media reports rather than an official internal an...

item.title: How Pop Music Inspired Writers to Explore Great Literature
item.description: Ahead of World Book Day, Guardian music writers reflect on how pop and rock musicians guided them to important works of literature. Katie Hawthorne recalls discovering Penelope Farmer’s 'Charlotte Som...

item.title: Australians safely repatriated from the Middle East amid ongoing conflict
item.description: The first group of Australians stranded in the Middle East due to escalating conflict has safely returned to Sydney, marking the beginning of a broader repatriation effort for over 115,000 Australians...
